Details:
Ultraviolet irradiation was found to be effective in degrading propoxur (up to 90% degradation) in one study; however, it produced a relatively minor degree of mineralization (about 16%) due to the formation of photo-Fries products.  Degradation of propoxur has been found to be effective in serveral studies with UV in the presence of a photocatalyst such as titanium dioxide, 2,4,6-triphenylpyrylium-Zeolite, zeolite supported TiO2 and GAC supported TiO2 (up to 100% degradation can be achieved).
The degradation of propoxur by UV radiation with a photocatalyst is generally influenced by pH, initial propoxur concentration, photocatalyst loading and UV light intensity. UV reaction times used were generally much longer than those used in typical drinking water applications of UV.
